active_tasks_found: SESSION_PNL_TRUE_START_FIX_001 (ACTIVE — task JSON confirmed, prerequisite MARKET_COOLDOWN_PERSIST_001 APPROVED)

exact_section_changed.location: main() startup block — existing cooldown restore block extended (lines 343-364)
exact_section_changed.old_header:
# ── Restore market cooldown from prior state ───────────────────────────
global _market_cooldown
exact_section_changed.new_header:
# ── Restore cooldown and session start from prior state ───────────────
global _market_cooldown, _session_start_ts
added_lines: # Restore session start so PnL accumulates from true session start, not restart

old_behavior: _session_start_ts was set to int(time.time()) at module load (line 108), so every restart reset it to the current time. session_pnl in both bot_core.py (session loss cap check) and dashboard_server.py (bankroll block) only counted trades closed since the last restart — not the full trading session.
new_behavior: On startup, main() reads pnl.session_start_ts from the prior state.json. If that value is within the last 24 hours (86400s), _session_start_ts is restored to it rather than the current time. session_pnl now accumulates from the original session start across all restarts within the same trading day. After 24 hours the value ages out and a new session starts fresh on the next restart.
how_session_start_is_determined: On first launch of the day (no prior state.json, or prior ts older than 24h): _session_start_ts = int(time.time()) — standard fresh session. On restart during the same day: _session_start_ts restored from pnl.session_start_ts in state.json, which was written by _write_state() on the prior run. The 24h window prevents stale carryover to the next trading day.
dashboard_server_impact: No change required. dashboard_server.py already reads session_start_ts from state.json pnl block (line 529) and queries the DB from that timestamp. Once bot_core.py writes the correct (sticky) session_start_ts to state.json, the dashboard picks it up automatically.

verification.py_compile_dashboard_server: Not run — file was not changed

restart_notes: Yes — restart required. The session_start_ts restore only runs in main() on startup. The fix has no effect on the currently running process. Additionally, the stale __pycache__/bot_core.cpython-*.pyc must be deleted before restart per OPERATOR_ACTION_REQUIRED_001.
summary: Extended the existing startup load block in main() to also restore _session_start_ts from prior state.json (if within 24h) so session PnL accumulates from the true session start rather than resetting to the last restart timestamp.
